metadata.title: States PCS: Jammu & Kashmir GK Questions for JKPSC Exams

questions.question: Who built the Martand Sun Temple in Jammu & Kashmir?
questions.explanation: Lalitaditya Muktapida of the Karkota dynasty constructed the Martand Sun Temple in the 8th century CE near Anantnag, Jammu & Kashmir. It is among the finest examples of early Hindu architecture.

questions.question: Which treaty in 1846 led to the formal establishment of the princely state of Jammu & Kashmir?
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Amritsar (1846) between the British and Maharaja Gulab Singh established Jammu & Kashmir as a princely state after payment to the British, initiating Dogra rule.

questions.question: Who is credited with firmly establishing Islam in Kashmir during the 14th century?
questions.explanation: Shah Mir founded Kashmir’s first Muslim dynasty in 1339, establishing Islamic rule and influence.

questions.question: Who authored the Persian chronicle ‘Baharistan-i-Shahi’, which records the medieval history of Kashmir?
questions.explanation: ‘Baharistan-i-Shahi’ is an anonymous Persian chronicle detailing Kashmir’s medieval history, particularly its Islamic-era rulers and events.

questions.question: Who was the Sikh ruler responsible for conquering Kashmir in 1819, which ended the Afghan rule in the region?
questions.explanation: Maharaja Ranjit Singh annexed Kashmir in 1819, ending the Durrani Afghan rule and bringing the region under Sikh Empire.

questions.question: Which uprising took place in Kashmir in 1931 against the Dogra monarchy?
questions.explanation: The July Revolt of 1931 was a mass uprising against Dogra rule, leading to political mobilization and establishment of Kashmiri civil rights movements.

questions.question: Which river forms the northernmost river valley in Jammu & Kashmir?
questions.explanation: Shyok River, a tributary of the Indus, flows through the northernmost part of the region, forming the highest river valleys in Ladakh (previously part of J&K).

questions.question: Which is the largest glacier in the Karakoram Range of Jammu & Kashmir?
questions.explanation: Siachen Glacier is the largest glacier in the Karakoram Range and India, located in eastern Karakoram in Jammu & Kashmir.

questions.question: Which district in Jammu & Kashmir is famously called the “Land of Saffron”?
questions.explanation: Pulwama district, particularly Pampore, is renowned for saffron cultivation due to favorable soil and climate.

questions.question: Which river flows through Poonch district in Jammu and Kashmir and merges with the Jhelum River in Pakistan?
questions.explanation: The Suran River passes through Poonch district, Jammu & Kashmir, and joins the Jhelum River in Pakistan.
